bullets.text: Ukrainian President Zelensky has dismissed the country's top army commander, Oleksandr Syrskyi, following days of protests related to the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

The US has launched new strikes on Iran, with President Trump warning of retaliation after the deaths of American soldiers. Iran claims to have hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz, as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.
A UN report indicates that a healthy diet remains too expensive for one in three people globally, despite a third consecutive year of decline in global hunger.
Hollywood star Rebel Wilson awaits a Federal Court judgment today in her defamation case against the lead actor of her film, which could clarify the boundaries of public commentary.
The mother of a homeless man found dead outside a Sydney train station, unnoticed for days, is appealing for her son's remains to be repatriated to Nepal.
Teachers in Victoria are set to strike this week after failing to reach an agreement with the state government regarding their ongoing dispute.
Moody's has upgraded Argentina's credit rating, marking the third sovereign upgrade in under three months, which validates President Javier Milei's economic overhaul plan.

Saudi tankers have reversed course following threats from Houthi rebels, as a new threat emerges for Red Sea shipping, with Goldman Sachs warning oil could jump to $120 per barrel if the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted.
Bitcoin has rallied as crypto markets show signs of movement, with indications that Washington is progressing towards a clearer regulatory framework for digital assets.
